title: Breakwave Bi-Weekly Dry Bulk Report - July 7, 2026
section_path: Breakwave Bi-Weekly Dry Bulk Report - July 7, 2026
section_path_text: Breakwave Bi-Weekly Dry Bulk Report - July 7, 2026
summary:
Main
• A Solid First Half for Dry Bulk Shipping - Notwithstanding prevailing market uncertainty, the dry bulk shipping sector achieved an exceptionally strong performance in the first half of the year, characterized by tight global vessel supply that drove average earnings across all asset classes well above historical benchmarks. Operational inefficiencies, extended voyage lengths, and bunker fueling dislocations stemming from disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz oil flow further constrained market capacity, a trend compounded by a heavy drydocking schedule for aging vessels built during the 2009-2011 vessel construction boom. This reduced effective supply, paired with healthy iron ore demand, sustained Capesize earnings at highly profitable levels exceeding $30,000 per day.

The Baltic Dry Index (BDI) measures the average spot rates for dry bulk freight with a sector weighting of 40% Capesize, 30% Panamax and 30% Supramax. The Breakwave Dry Futures Index (BDRYFF) is designed to track freight futures contracts with a sector weighting of 50% Capesize, 40% Panamax and 10%
Supramax and a weighted average maturity of approximately 50-70 days.
